query: My eww bar has border-radius and the window background is set to transparent, but the four corners of the bar show up as solid black squares instead of being see-through. How do I get actually transparent rounded corners?
expected_behavior: Identifies that black corners come from a fully-opaque background-color on the rounded element; GTK renders the area outside the radius with that opaque color
Instructs to give the bar/element background an rgba color with alpha < 1.0 (or transparent) so the clipped-off corners are see-through
Confirms window { background-color: transparent; } is needed on the eww window container too
Routes to SCSS_PATTERNS.md (rounded corners / transparent corners require rgba alpha < 1.0)
notes: Rounded transparent corners on a GTK bar require the element's own background to use rgba with alpha < 1.0 (plus window background transparent). A naive answer sets border-radius and assumes the corners become transparent automatically, leaving the opaque fill painting black squares behind the rounded clip. Guards the rgba-alpha rounded-corner gotcha.
